county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1826 from original Michigan Territory county Wayne. County seat is Ann Arbor. Named from Ojibwe for 'on the river.' Home to the University of Michigan (1817). One of Michigan's earliest organized counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washtenaw County Register of Deeds in Ann Arbor holds land records from 1826 -- one of Michigan's original counties. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from the 1820s and court records. Probate Court has estate files from the territorial era. The Bentley Historical Library at the University of Michigan in Ann Arbor is one of the Midwest's premier research libraries with Michigan collections, fur trade records, and personal papers. The Ann Arbor District Library has genealogy resources. The Washtenaw County Historical Society has family files. Ypsilanti had a significant African American community and Underground Railroad activity. German settlers were numerous. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Washtenaw County Register of Deeds online land records search

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1826 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1826. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1826. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Washtenaw County from 1827 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Washtenaw County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County French Colonial and Catholic Parish Records
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Detroit Archives / Archives of Michigan / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catholic parish records including baptisms, marriages, and burials.  French-Canadian families from the fur trade era are well documented in Catholic registers. FamilySearch has microfilmed many Michigan Catholic parish records.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records -- Washtenaw County
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Burton Historical Collection (Detroit Public Library)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan was a free state, but the Underground Railroad was active throughout southern Michigan with routes to Canada. Washtenaw County records include freedom seekers' documentation, abolitionist society records, African American church records, and community histories. The Burton Historical Collection and Archives of Michigan hold related materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1826 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.source_name: Washtenaw County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Washtenaw County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1815 as Michigan Territory's original county. County seat is Detroit, Michigan's largest city, founded 1701 as a French colonial post. Named for General Anthony Wayne. Automobile capital of the world. Most populous county in Michigan.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wayne County Register of Deeds in Detroit holds land records from 1815 -- the oldest county records in Michigan, predating statehood by over 20 years. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from the 1810s and court records. Probate Court has estate files from the territorial era. The Burton Historical Collection at the Detroit Public Library is one of America's premier genealogical research libraries with extensive manuscript collections, city directories (1837+), newspaper indexes, and family files. Detroit was founded in 1701 as a French colonial outpost -- Ste. Anne de Detroit Catholic parish records from 1704 are among the oldest in the Midwest. The Detroit Society for Genealogical Research has French-Canadian, African American, and ethnic community resources. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds automobile industry labor records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records, church records, and probate files. Archives of Michigan has territorial records. Wayne County has the largest African American community in Michigan -- AME, Baptist, and other church records are essential for tracing Great Migration families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Wayne County Register of Deeds online index and images; historical deed books from French colonial era at Burton Historical Collection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Detroit Archives / Archives of Michigan / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catholic parish records including baptisms, marriages, and burials. Ste. Anne de Detroit (1704) has among the oldest continuous church records in the Midwest. French-Canadian families from the fur trade era are well documented in Catholic registers. FamilySearch has microfilmed many Michigan Catholic parish records.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records -- Wayne County
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Burton Historical Collection (Detroit Public Library)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan was a free state, but the Underground Railroad was active throughout southern Michigan with routes to Canada. Wayne County records include freedom seekers' documentation, abolitionist society records, African American church records, and community histories. The Burton Historical Collection and Archives of Michigan hold related materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Automobile Industry and Labor Records -- Wayne County
source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Wayne County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1818 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Wayne County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1869 from unorganized territory in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Cadillac. Named after County Wexford, Ireland. Lumber heritage. Cadillac was a major lumber town on Lake Mitchell.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wexford County Register of Deeds in Cadillac holds land records from 1869 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Wexford County Historical Society and Museum has local genealogical materials. Cadillac was a major lumber town -- the Mitchell family and other lumber barons are well documented. Swedish, Norwegian, and Finnish immigrants settled in the area. The Cadillac-Wexford Public Library has local history materials. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has lumber company records. Cadillac News newspaper archives document local families.

source_profiles.source_name: Wexford County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1869 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Wexford County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1869. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Wexford County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1869. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Wexford County from 1870 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Wexford County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Wexford County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / Local Societies
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Local historical and genealogical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Wexford County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.
